untranslatable_reason: The inverted cup and handle pattern requires detection of a specific price shape/formation (inverted cup geometry, handle formation, and downward breakout). This cannot be expressed using only the allowed point-in-time indicators and operators. Pattern recognition requires historical shape analysis, which is beyond the scope of individual bar indicators. The 'breakout downward from handle' condition also requires identification of the handle's upper boundary, which is a structural feature not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: Approximated entry conditions using volume trend (downward) and price declining below previous close as proxy signals for downward breakout momentum. Exit uses price crossing above SMA_20 as reversal signal since AUTHOR_SILENT on specific exit levels. The actual pattern detection (cup shape, handle formation) cannot be implemented without pattern recognition capabilities. Success rates vary significantly: bear market (rank 1/21) shows 26% average decline vs bull market (rank 8/21) shows 16% decline, suggesting better performance in downtrends.
